subject: DevOps
title: Security Champions Program
description: A micro-lesson that makes Security Champions Program usable.

hook.text: One security team can't review all code for 500 developers. The math fails. You need multipliers.

buildup.text: A champions program embeds security-minded devs in each team. They're the local security experts.

discovery.text: Champions get extra training and bridge their team to the security team. They review PRs for security.

twist.text: Champions don't replace the security team. They amplify it. Common questions get answered faster.

climax.text: Microsoft's champions program scaled security practices across thousands of engineers effectively.

punchline.text: Embed security in every team. Champions scale the culture.

quiz.question: What role does a security champion play within a development team?
options: They act as a local security expert and bridge to the security team
They replace the central security team entirely
